items.definition_en: A sofa is a comfortable seat for several people.
items.example_en: We sat together on the sofa to watch TV.

items.definition_en: A table is a piece of furniture with a flat top and legs.
items.example_en: I put my books on the table in the living room.

items.definition_en: A chair is something you sit on.
items.example_en: She pulled out a chair and sat down.

items.definition_en: A bed is a piece of furniture where you sleep.
items.example_en: He lay down on the bed and fell asleep.

items.definition_en: A wardrobe is a large cupboard for keeping clothes.
items.example_en: I hang my shirts in the wardrobe.

items.definition_en: A cupboard is a piece of furniture with shelves and doors.
items.example_en: We keep the plates in the kitchen cupboard.

items.definition_en: A shelf is a flat board for putting things on.
items.example_en: Books are arranged neatly on the shelf.

items.definition_en: A desk is a table you use for working or studying.
items.example_en: My computer is on my desk.

items.definition_en: An armchair is a comfortable chair with sides for your arms.
items.example_en: Grandpa likes to read in his armchair.

items.definition_en: A dresser is a chest with drawers for clothes.
items.example_en: She put her socks in the dresser.

items.definition_en: A bookshelf is a piece of furniture for holding books.
items.example_en: The bookshelf is full of my favorite novels.

items.definition_en: A nightstand is a small table beside a bed.
items.example_en: I keep a lamp on my nightstand.

items.definition_en: A rocking chair is a chair that moves back and forth.
items.example_en: My grandmother sits in the rocking chair by the window.

items.definition_en: A stool is a seat with no back or arms.
items.example_en: He sat on the stool in the kitchen.

items.definition_en: A fireplace is a heating device that burns wood or other fuel.
items.example_en: We gathered around the fireplace on cold evenings.
